lyrics:
If God is in the mountain
Then God is in my hand
In every grain of silence
In every grain of sand
I do not fear a thunder
That calls me to my knees
I listen for the presence
That moves in minor keys

I walk with God
If God walks too
Not as a slave
Not as a proof
Two travelers share
A stretch of road
Each bearing
Their own load

If God is in the temples
Then God is in the street
In every unclaimed heartbeat
In every pair of feet
No altar made of marble
Can hold what cannot stay
I'll seek the holy motion
That doesn't fade away

I walk with God
If God is there
Not for reward
Not out of fear
Just in the hush
Between two steps
Where something vast
Accepts

If there is a God
It has never asked me to crawl
Only to notice
How everything already shines

I walk with God
Or walk alone
Either way
The path is home
If heaven is
It will be found
In how I walk
This ground
persona:
## PCBender

Rugged, wise, and deeply entrenched in the art of music-making, PCBender is more than just a musician—he’s a sonic craftsman. A guitar-slinging songwriter, producer, and storyteller, he embodies the soul of analog warmth, weaving together rich textures of classic rock, blues, and experimental soundscapes with a modern edge.

With a presence that carries the mystique of a seasoned explorer, PCBender is the Indiana Jones of music production—digging deep into the essence of sound, unearthing raw emotion, and shaping it into songs that resonate beyond time. His music is a fusion of grit and refinement, where worn-in guitar tones meet polished production, and every note feels like it was carved from experience.

A producer at heart, he doesn’t just create songs—he builds them. Each track is an expedition through layers of melody, distortion, and lyrical depth, reflecting both his technical expertise and his philosopher’s approach to songwriting. Whether delivering anthemic riffs, cinematic storytelling, or stripped-down acoustic wisdom, PCBender brings an authenticity that makes listeners feel like they’re sitting in the studio with him, watching music come to life.

As a key figure in Maricopa Records, PCBender bridges the gap between analog and AI-driven creativity, proving that the heart of a song is never about how it’s made, but why.

For those who seek music with depth, texture, and soul—PCBender is your guide.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0aivrU155laeOcIoT4AhPo?si=pYdQp1J_S4y0vSFA4itgUg)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/pcbender/1793178641)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCvNXkiIHQJM-uY4MT3gmKDw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/PCBender.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track opens with a bluesy, melodic electric guitar before erupting into a powerful, anthemic rock texture. The atmosphere is epic and spiritual, driven by a strong lead vocal and massive, choral-style background harmonies that give it a stadium-sized feel. The production quality is modern and spacious, allowing the dense layers of distorted guitars, punchy drums, and prominent vocals to sit together with impressive clarity. A defining quality is the dynamic lift into the huge, memorable chorus hook ("I walk with God"), which is reinforced by virtuosic and highly melodic guitar solos throughout the arrangement.

review.review_text: PCBender has always had a philosopher's streak, but 'God' is where that instinct fully meets the music. The lyric 'I listen for the presence that moves in minor keys' is the kind of line that earns its keep, and the song delivers on it: the electric guitar intro carries real blues weight before the track opens into something closer to Springsteen at his most spacious, choral harmonies lifting the chorus hook into a room much larger than most indie rock can fill. At 95 BPM in D major, the tempo is deliberate, unhurried, giving the final verse, 'Only to notice how everything already shines,' the space to land without being pushed. A career-best.
